message:
fcp-mesh: require Ed25519 peer signatures on gossip summaries, revocation pushes, decode status, and symbol acks

Add control-plane signature verification to the gossip message
handlers so MeshNode no longer accepts state-mutating peer input on
trust. All four paths (`GossipSummary`, `RevocationPushMessage`,
`DecodeStatus`, `SymbolAck`) now require the sender's registered
Ed25519 key to sign a canonical transcript of the message before the
handler applies any state change or metric update.

crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs:

* `GossipSummary::verify_signature(&Ed25519VerifyingKey) -> Result<(), CryptoError>`:
  returns `CryptoError::VerificationFailed` when the summary is
  unsigned; otherwise reconstructs the attached
  `Ed25519Signature` from `signature.signature` bytes and calls
  `verifying_key.verify(&self.signing_bytes(), &signature)`.
* `RevocationPushMessage::signing_bytes()`:
  deterministic transcript with a domain-separator
  (`b"FCP2-REVOCATION-PUSH-V1"`) followed by length-prefixed
  `from`, `zone_id`, a length-prefixed sequence of 32-byte
  `ObjectId`s, then `new_rev_seq` (u64 LE) and `timestamp` (u64 LE).
  Marked `#[must_use]` with a `# Panics` note that the `u32::try_from`
  on each length falls back to `u32::MAX` via `unwrap_or`, so the
  function does not actually panic — it produces a non-ambiguous
  saturating encoding for pathological inputs.
* `RevocationPushMessage::verify_signature` mirrors the summary
  variant against the same canonical-transcript pattern.
* `MeshGossip::summary_ttl_secs() -> u64` exposes the configured
  summary-freshness window so MeshNode can enforce push freshness
  without reaching into `GossipConfig` directly.

crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:

* New `MeshNodeError` variants:
  - `PeerSigningKeyMissing { peer }` — registry has no key for peer.
  - `PeerSignatureInvalid { peer, message_kind }` — signature failed
    to validate OR the message was unsigned; `message_kind` is one
    of `"gossip summary"`, `"revocation push"`, `"decode status"`,
    `"symbol ack"` so operators see which control-plane surface
    rejected the input.
  - `SignatureNodeMismatch { message_kind, expected, actual }` —
    the signature's embedded `node_id` disagrees with the message's
    `from` field (attempted attacker rebinding a victim's signature
    onto their own message).
* New `VerifiedRevocationPush` struct returned by the dispatcher:
  the same payload as `RevocationPushMessage` minus the signature
  (already verified), for callers that own the revocation registry.
* Helpers:
  - `peer_signing_key(&NodeId) -> Result<&Ed25519VerifyingKey,
    MeshNodeError>`.
  - `verify_summary_signature(&GossipSummary) -> Result<NodeId, _>`:
    checks signature present → checks `signature.node_id ==
    summary.from` → looks up peer key → verifies signature. Returns
    the verified `NodeId`.
  - `verify_revocation_push_signature(&RevocationPushMessage) ->
    Result<NodeId, _>`: same shape as summary.
* New public methods:
  - `handle_summary(GossipSummary, now_secs)` — verify then dispatch
    to `self.gossip.handle_summary`; bumps `metrics.gossip_updates`
    only on success.
  - `handle_revocation_push(RevocationPushMessage, now_secs) ->
    Result<VerifiedRevocationPush, _>` — verify signature, then
    reject as stale if `now_secs - push.timestamp >
    self.gossip.summary_ttl_secs()` (using the same TTL window as
    summaries, which prevents replay of old pushes). Returns the
    unsigned descriptor for the caller's registry.
  - `handle_gossip_message(GossipMessage, now_secs) ->
    Result<Option<VerifiedRevocationPush>, _>` — single entry point
    that routes `Summary`/`RevocationPush` through the verifying
    handlers and silently ignores `Request`/`Response`/
    `ReconcileRequest`/`ReconcileResponse` (those don't mutate state
    yet and stay unsigned for now).
* Breaking change to the DecodeStatus/SymbolAck handlers:
  - `handle_decode_status(&mut self, peer: &NodeId, status:
    &DecodeStatus, now_ms: u64) -> Result<(), MeshNodeError>` now
    looks up the peer's signing key and calls `status.verify(key)`
    before `symbol_requests.process_decode_status(...)`.
  - `handle_symbol_ack(...)` gains the same `peer: &NodeId`
    parameter and signature check, and only then processes the ack,
    records the metric, and removes the object from `sent_symbols`.
  Callers must now pass the peer that produced the message; passing
  an unknown peer or one whose signature doesn't match returns
  `PeerSigningKeyMissing` / `PeerSignatureInvalid`.

Threat model covered:
- Unsigned messages are rejected before any state change.
- Signatures bound to a different node_id than the message's `from`
  are rejected (prevents replay-under-different-identity).
- Stale revocation pushes are rejected using the gossip TTL.
- Unknown senders cannot advance symbol/decode state without first
  registering a signing key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
